free_text: of two individual irradiations with different initial
were performed at the TSL during each of which three to
to four different stacks were irradiated separately
Up to 30 small stacks from high-purity materials with
diam. of 15.0 mm were 55 or 125 mg/cm**2 for lead
(99.99% purity) and 10 or 33 mg/cm**2 for aluminum
(99.999% purity). In general, each unit consisted of
three aluminum foils and three to seven lead foils.
Only one or more of the inner Al respectively Pb foils
were used for the measurements to avoid cross
with automatic sample changers were used. Typical
resolutions ranged from about 1 keV at 122 keV of Co-57
to about 2 keV at 1332 keV of Co-60.
The irradiations were performed in air and lasted
typically about twelve hours to get sufficient fluence.
support. For details of the experimental setup and
parameters of the experiments at LNS see Table 1 of
After end of irradiations the targets were
transported to Cologne were the stacks and mini-stacks
were dismounted and the individual targets were
separated.  A part of them stayed at Cologne for
measurement, another one was transported to Hannover
for the same purpose. The gamma-spectrometric
measurements started about between 36 and 60 hours
after the end of irradiation, respectively.
Up to 30 measurements were made of each lead target
during a time span of up to two years after irradiation
During this time each sample was measured on a minimum
of five different detectors by this way avoiding
systematic errors. Measurement times ranged from five
minutes in the beginning up to about three days in
some cases at the end of a measurement series.
In some cases where we irradiated more than three lead
targets at one energy point two adjacent targets were
measured together to get better counting statistics.
* Uncertainties due to fluctuations in the beam
* Uncertainty of irradiation time, decay time and
* Uncertainty of impurities - due to the high purity
of the target foils contributions of other constituents
* Uncertainty of nuclide production by secondary
* The dead-time of the detector systems was
uncertainty of the determination of net
abundances were also taken from REL-REF.
Uncertainty of number of irradiated nuclei
No uncertainties were attributed to the monitor
While absorption of gamma-quanta in the aluminum
catcher foils played no role, it did in the lead foils.
Since we evaluated our spectra for energies down to
95 keV we had to apply corrections for this effect.
-By authors- A cross section for the production of a
nuclide is denoted as independent if the nuclide can
only be produced directly via the nuclear reaction
between the projectile and the target nucleus and not
via subsequent beta-,beta+,EC- or alpha- decays. Such
independent cross sections are obtained if:
* either the nuclide is shielded by stable nucleus
against beta-decay or by a long-lived progenitor,
* or the cross section for the production of a
progenitor is also measured so that the production via
Data tables were received from the authors on
diskette. Prelim. data presented in Appendix C.4 of
ERR-ANALYS update. BIB update (free text was reduced).
